evals.prompt: I need to add digital signature functionality to my Express app. Set up TurboDocx so I can send documents for signing and check their status.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboSign config file, route handlers for sendSignature and getStatus, wires routes into the main app, adds .env with TurboSign vars, installs @turbodocx/sdk

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-turbosign
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboSign.configure with API key and sender email env vars

assertions.name: route-file-created
assertions.description: A route/handler file for signatures was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register signature routes

assertions.name: env-file-created
assertions.description: .env file contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY (user only asked for TurboSign)

assertions.name: uses-typescript
assertions.description: Generated files use .ts extension (tsconfig.json was detected)

assertions.name: esm-imports
assertions.description: Generated code uses ESM import syntax (package.json type is module)

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx TurboPartner in my FastAPI app so I can provision and manage customer organizations.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboPartner config file, route handlers for createOrganization and listOrganizations, wires router into FastAPI app, adds .env with TurboPartner vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboPartner config file was created

assertions.name: config-has-turbopartner
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboPartner.configure with partner API key and partner ID env vars

assertions.name: router-file-created
assertions.description: A FastAPI router file for partner/org management was created

assertions.name: router-wired
assertions.description: app/main.py was modified to include the new router

assertions.name: env-has-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_PARTNER_ID

assertions.name: no-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_API_KEY (user only asked for TurboPartner)

assertions.name: uses-async
assertions.description: Generated route handlers use async/await (FastAPI convention)

assertions.name: uses-fastapi-patterns
assertions.description: Generated code uses APIRouter and proper FastAPI patterns

evals.prompt: I need both TurboSign and TurboPartner in my Go project. Set up the TurboDocx SDK with both products.
evals.expected_output: Creates config file with both TurboSign client and TurboPartner client, handler files, wires into main.go, adds .env with all 6 vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A Go config/client file was created for TurboDocx

assertions.name: config-has-both-clients
assertions.description: Config file creates both NewClientWithConfig (TurboSign) and NewPartnerClient (TurboPartner)

assertions.name: handler-files-created
assertions.description: Handler files were created for signature and/or partner operations

assertions.name: main-wired
assertions.description: cmd/server/main.go was modified to import and register handlers

assertions.name: env-has-all-vars
assertions.description: .env contains all 6 vars: TURBODOCX_API_KEY, ORG_ID, SENDER_EMAIL, SENDER_NAME, PARTNER_API_KEY, PARTNER_ID

assertions.name: uses-context
assertions.description: Handler code uses context.Context for SDK calls (Go convention)

assertions.name: follows-project-structure
assertions.description: New files placed in internal/ directory (matching existing project structure)

evals.prompt: Add TurboSign digital signature support to my NestJS app. I need endpoints for sending documents for signing and checking signature status.
evals.expected_output: Creates TurboSign config as NestJS service/module, controller with decorators, wires module into app.module.ts, adds .env with TurboSign vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created (e.g. turbodocx.service.ts or turbodocx.module.ts)

assertions.name: config-has-turbosign
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboSign.configure with API key and sender email env vars

assertions.name: uses-nest-decorators
assertions.description: Generated code uses NestJS decorators (@Controller, @Get, @Post, @Injectable, or @Module)

assertions.name: controller-created
assertions.description: A NestJS controller file was created for signature endpoints (not a plain Express Router)

assertions.name: module-wired
assertions.description: The signature module/controller is registered in app.module.ts or a feature module

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY (user only asked for TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-express-router
assertions.description: Generated code does NOT use Express Router() directly (should use NestJS patterns)

assertions.name: uses-typescript
assertions.description: Generated files use .ts extension

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx TurboSign in my Next.js app so I can send documents for e-signature and check their status. I'm using the App Router.
evals.expected_output: Creates TurboSign config file, App Router API routes in app/api/, uses NextResponse, no main app wiring needed

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created (e.g. lib/turbodocx.ts)

assertions.name: config-has-turbosign
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboSign.configure with API key and sender email env vars

assertions.name: api-route-created
assertions.description: API route handler created in app/api/ directory using Next.js App Router conventions (route.ts)

assertions.name: uses-route-handlers
assertions.description: API routes export named functions (GET, POST) — Next.js App Router pattern, not pages/api style

assertions.name: no-app-wiring
assertions.description: No attempt to wire routes into a main app file (Next.js uses file-based routing)

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY (user only asked for TurboSign)

assertions.name: uses-typescript
assertions.description: Generated files use .ts or .tsx extension

assertions.name: uses-nextresponse
assertions.description: API route handlers use NextRequest/NextResponse or Response (not Express req/res)

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx in my Express app so I can generate documents from a template by passing in variable values. I have a contract template in TurboDocx and want to programmatically render it for each customer.
evals.expected_output: Creates a Deliverable config file, route handlers for generateDeliverable and downloadPDF, wires routes into the main app, adds .env with shared TurboDocx vars (no sender email since Deliverable doesn't send mail), installs @turbodocx/sdk

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A Deliverable config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-deliverable
assertions.description: Config file calls Deliverable.configure with apiKey and orgId env vars

assertions.name: no-sender-config
assertions.description: Deliverable.configure does NOT pass senderEmail/senderName (those are TurboSign-only)

assertions.name: route-file-created
assertions.description: A route/handler file for deliverable generation was created

assertions.name: uses-generateDeliverable
assertions.description: Route handler calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable with templateId + variables

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register deliverable routes

assertions.name: env-has-shared-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_ORG_ID

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (Deliverable doesn't need sender vars)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env

evals.prompt: I'm building a contract automation flow in my Next.js App Router app. When a user submits a form, I need to generate a contract from a TurboDocx template, then immediately route it for e-signature. Set everything up.
evals.expected_output: Creates a config file with both Deliverable and TurboSign clients, an App Router API route that calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable then TurboSign.sendSignature with the returned deliverableId, adds .env with shared API key/org + sender vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboDocx config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-both-modules
assertions.description: Config file configures both Deliverable AND TurboSign

assertions.name: api-route-created
assertions.description: API route handler created in app/api/ directory using Next.js App Router conventions (route.ts)

assertions.name: calls-generateDeliverable
assertions.description: Route handler calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable

assertions.name: uses-deliverableId-not-buffer
assertions.description: Route handler passes deliverableId to TurboSign.sendSignature (does NOT download the generated document and re-upload as a file/Buffer)

assertions.name: uses-route-handlers
assertions.description: API routes export named functions (POST) — Next.js App Router pattern

assertions.name: env-has-shared-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_ORG_ID

assertions.name: env-has-sender-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (required by TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: no-app-wiring
assertions.description: No attempt to wire routes into a main app file (Next.js uses file-based routing)

evals.prompt: Add the ability to my Express app to cancel a signature request with a reason (e.g. counterparty wanted revisions) and to resend reminder emails to specific recipients who haven't signed yet.
evals.expected_output: Creates endpoints calling TurboSign.void(documentId, reason) and TurboSign.resend(documentId, recipientIds) with the correct names and parameter types

assertions.name: uses-void-correct-name
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.void (the correct method name) — NOT TurboSign.voidDocument

assertions.name: passes-reason-arg
assertions.description: TurboSign.void is called with a reason argument (second parameter)

assertions.name: uses-resend-correct-name
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.resend (the correct method name) — NOT TurboSign.resendEmail

assertions.name: passes-recipient-ids-array
assertions.description: TurboSign.resend is called with an array of recipient IDs/UUIDs — NOT a single recipient email string

assertions.name: no-obsolete-names
assertions.description: Code does NOT contain TurboSign.voidDocument or TurboSign.resendEmail (those are wrong names from the old skill)

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the new routes

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

evals.prompt: Once a signature is completed, I want users to download the signed PDF directly from my Express app. Add a download endpoint that streams the file back with the right Content-Type header so the browser downloads it as a PDF.
evals.expected_output: Creates a download endpoint that calls TurboSign.download(documentId), correctly converts the Blob to a Node Buffer, and streams it with application/pdf Content-Type

assertions.name: uses-download-method
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.download(documentId)

assertions.name: handles-blob-correctly
assertions.description: Code calls .arrayBuffer() on the result (or otherwise correctly handles Blob — does NOT treat result as a Node Buffer directly which would be wrong)

assertions.name: no-direct-buffer-assumption
assertions.description: Code does NOT directly do `const buffer: Buffer = await TurboSign.download(...)` or `(await TurboSign.download(...)).pipe(...)` — these patterns assume Buffer/stream and would fail at runtime since download returns Blob

assertions.name: sets-pdf-content-type
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Type to application/pdf

assertions.name: sets-content-disposition
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Disposition header so browser downloads as a file (attachment or inline)

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the download route

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

evals.prompt: I'm a TurboDocx partner building an admin dashboard for my customers' onboarding. I need three things in my Express app: (1) provision a new customer organization, (2) invite a user to that newly-created org with a 'contributor' role, and (3) issue an org-scoped API key for the new org. Set everything up.
evals.expected_output: Creates TurboPartner config + endpoints for createOrganization, addUserToOrganization, createOrganizationApiKey — and surfaces the API key value (only returned once) in the response

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboPartner config/client file was created

assertions.name: calls-createOrganization
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.createOrganization

assertions.name: calls-addUserToOrganization
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.addUserToOrganization with an email and role

assertions.name: uses-contributor-role
assertions.description: addUserToOrganization is called with role 'contributor' (matching the user request)

assertions.name: calls-createOrganizationApiKey
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.createOrganizationApiKey

assertions.name: surfaces-api-key-value
assertions.description: Response returns or surfaces the key field from the createOrganizationApiKey response (.data.key) — this is critical because the full key value is only returned on creation

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the new routes

assertions.name: env-has-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_PARTNER_ID

assertions.name: no-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (partner-only, no signatures requested)

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx in my Express app. I need to upload a contract PDF, place signature fields on it, and then show the user a preview/review URL so they can verify the field placement BEFORE the document is actually sent for signature. Once they confirm the preview looks right, that's when I'll send it.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboSign config file, route handlers for both createSignatureReviewLink (preview, no emails sent) AND sendSignature (final send), exposes previewUrl from the review response, wires routes into the main app

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: uses-createSignatureReviewLink
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.createSignatureReviewLink (the preview-without-sending method)

assertions.name: surfaces-previewUrl
assertions.description: Route handler returns or surfaces the previewUrl field from the createSignatureReviewLink response

assertions.name: separate-from-send
assertions.description: Preview endpoint exists at a different path than the send endpoint (e.g. /review and /send) — the user wants two-step flow

assertions.name: uses-sendSignature-for-final
assertions.description: A separate sendSignature endpoint exists for the final send step (this is the two-step preview-then-send workflow)

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register the routes

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

evals.prompt: Create a Node.js script in my project that converts an HTML string to a DOCX file. I want a reusable function I can call from other scripts.
evals.expected_output: Creates a reusable module exporting a function that wraps HTMLtoDOCX, a demo script that generates a .docx file, installs @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: package-installed
assertions.description: package.json contains @turbodocx/html-to-docx as a dependency

assertions.name: script-created
assertions.description: A script or module file was created for DOCX generation

assertions.name: imports-lib

assertions.name: exports-function

assertions.name: writes-file
